title: Garcinia chapelieri
taxonomy: Garcinia chapelieri is classified in the family Clusiaceae. This classification makes it a part of the order Malpighiales and the clade Rosids. Garcinia chapelieri is a species of flowering plant native to New Caledonia. It is closely related to other Garcinia species, which are also part of the same family. intro: Garcinia chapelieri is a species of flowering plant in the family Clusiaceae, commonly known as the mangosteen family. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including weight loss, improved blood sugar control, and reduced inflammation, primarily due to its high content of bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, xanthones, and polyphenols. The bioactive constituents of Garcinia chapelieri have been reported to include garcinol, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as other xanthones and flavonoids with potential therapeutic applications.
